name: Missed Revenue Calculator
tagline: Calculate how much ₹ your team is leaving on the table each week from stale leads.
description:
Enter your team size, avg deal value per industry, grade distribution, and weekly stale-lead count. The calculator returns your ₹ at risk — the same metric Leadkaun surfaces automatically every Monday for teams running the system.

Use it once, and you'll understand why ₹-at-risk is the metric most sales reviews should open with.
inside: Avg deal value benchmarks across 6 Indian B2B industries
Grade-weighted conversion rates (Grade A, B, C)
Formula: avg_deal × conv_rate × stale_count = ₹ at risk
Comparison of before/after Leadkaun adoption for similar teams
howToUse: Open the calculator (embedded below).
Enter team size, avg deal value, weekly stale-lead estimate.
See the ₹ at risk — bring this number to Monday's sales review.
whyItMatters: Most Indian SMB sales teams lose ₹2–5 lakh per week to stale leads and don't see it. The calculator translates 'stale lead' from operations speak into business speak — one number your CFO and sales manager will both track.

name: Lead Scoring Google Sheet Template
tagline: A plug-and-play Google Sheet for grading leads A–F manually — start with 50 leads, expand as needed.
description:
The fastest way to adopt lead scoring is to start manual. This template ships with Fit/Intent/Quality columns, a grade matrix formula, a rank score column, and a priority-queue tab — ready to use with your own lead list.

After 2 weeks of manual use, you'll have recovered ₹1–3 lakh and be ready to automate with Leadkaun.
inside: Fit/Intent/Quality scoring columns (0–100 each)
Grade matrix formula (auto-assigns A–F)
Rank score formula for Priority Queue ordering
SLA bands by grade (24h / 48h / 7d)
Worked example with 20 sample real-estate leads
howToUse: Make a copy of the Google Sheet (open in your own Drive).
Paste your own open leads into the Leads tab.
Score each by hand — 50 leads takes ~90 minutes first time.
Work the Priority Queue top-down every morning for 2 weeks.
Measure ₹ recovered vs baseline.
whyItMatters: Manual lead scoring proves the behaviour before you buy any tool. If your team won't work a manually-scored priority queue, they won't work an automated one either. Test cheap before spending.

name: Morning Brief Email Template
tagline: A ready-to-edit email template for your 8:30 AM IST sales brief — per rep, per manager.
description: If you're not using Leadkaun yet but want to build the Morning Brief ritual, this template covers both the rep version and the manager version. Adapt the merge tags to your own stack.
inside: Rep-version template (₹ at risk today, top 5 Grade A leads, overdue follow-ups)
Manager-version template (per-rep rollup, team ₹ at risk, coaching hooks)
Subject-line patterns that get 80%+ open rate
Merge-tag cheatsheet (lead_name, rep_name, ₹_at_risk, grade)
howToUse: Copy the markdown template into your email tool (Gmail, Outlook, Resend).
Replace merge tags with your system's placeholders.
Schedule for 8:25–8:35 AM IST, Monday–Saturday.
Iterate copy for 2 weeks based on open/click metrics.
whyItMatters: The Morning Brief is what turns lead scoring into a daily habit. Even without Leadkaun, you can run a simplified version in a day — the behaviour change is worth more than the tool.

name: ICP Definition Worksheet
tagline: A structured worksheet to define your Ideal Customer Profile in under 90 minutes.
description:
Most Indian SMBs can't articulate their ICP beyond 'B2B' or 'Indian companies'. This worksheet walks through the 5 dimensions that matter most — industry, geography, company size, role, budget — with prompts that force specificity.

The output is a 1-page ICP doc your whole sales team can use to grade leads consistently.
inside: 5-dimension ICP framework (Industry, Geography, Size, Role, Budget)
Prompt questions that surface hidden assumptions
Weighting guide for Fit Score configuration
Examples from 6 Indian B2B verticals (real estate, EdTech, BFSI, SaaS, manufacturing, healthcare)
ICP validation checklist
howToUse: Set aside 90 minutes with founder + sales lead.
Work through the 5 dimensions one by one, with examples.
Validate against last 10 closed-won deals — does your ICP match them?
Share the 1-page output with your sales team.
whyItMatters: Without an ICP, 'qualified lead' is a gut feel. With an ICP, it's a repeatable decision. Every rep grading the same lead arrives at the same grade.

name: Weekly Sales Review Template
tagline: A 30-minute Monday-morning review structure — anchored on ₹ at risk and ₹ recovered, not activity.
description:
Most sales reviews are activity theatre. This template restructures them around money: open with ₹ at risk, per-rep rollup, identify stuck leads, end with specific ₹ recovery commitments for the week.

Teams that adopt this rhythm shift to behaviour-based accountability in 8–12 weeks.
inside: 30-minute agenda (opening, per-rep, escalations, coaching, commitment)
Per-rep rollup template
Commitment-round script
Manager prep checklist for the Sunday before
Red-flag patterns to watch for in the first 4 weeks
howToUse: Block 30 min every Monday at 10 AM.
Sunday prep: pull ₹ at risk per rep, recent misses.
Run the template top-down. Don't exceed 30 min.
Follow up on commitments in Friday's end-of-week check.
whyItMatters: A review that opens with 'how many calls?' sets the wrong frame for the whole meeting. A review that opens with 'team ₹ at risk is ₹4.2L this week' anchors the team on outcomes.

name: WhatsApp B2B Sales Templates Pack
tagline: 25 ready-to-copy WhatsApp message templates by stage × industry — calibrated for Indian B2B.
description:
Reps spend too much time composing WhatsApp messages from scratch. This pack has 25 templates organised by sales stage (first reply, asking questions, objection, re-engagement, meeting booked) and industry (real estate, EdTech, BFSI, B2B SaaS, manufacturing).

Copy, customise the merge tags, send.
inside: 5 first-reply templates × 5 industries = 25 total
Objection-handling variants for price, timing, fit concerns
Re-engagement scripts for ghosted leads (Grade B → A recovery)
Meeting-booking follow-up templates
Compliance-safe language for BFSI (IRDAI-respectful)
howToUse: Browse by industry or sales stage.
Copy the template into WhatsApp, replace merge tags ({lead_name}, {product}, {rep_name}).
Send. Log the interaction in Leadkaun via 3-tap flow.
whyItMatters: A rep who types every message from scratch averages 90 seconds per WhatsApp reply. A rep using templates averages 15 seconds. Multiply by 30 interactions/day × 5 days/week = 3 hours/week of rep time recovered.

name: Sales Rep Performance Tracker (Google Sheet)
tagline: Track per-rep ₹ at risk, ₹ recovered, Grade A response time, and follow-up score weekly.
description:
A Google Sheet template for managers running performance tracking manually before adopting Leadkaun. Captures the 4 metrics that matter: ₹ at risk, ₹ recovered, Grade A response time, follow-up completion rate.

Auto-generates a per-rep scorecard and a team heatmap.
inside: Per-rep weekly entry tab (6 metrics)
Auto-rollup to team dashboard tab
Heatmap visualisation of Grade A response time
Rolling 4-week trend view
Coaching-conversation prompt cards keyed to each metric
howToUse: Make a copy of the sheet.
Enter week-1 baseline: for each rep, current ₹ at risk + last-week recovery.
Each Friday, each rep updates their tab.
Monday 10 AM review uses the rollup.
whyItMatters: Manual trackers teach the discipline. Once the team runs on ₹-at-risk conversations for 4 weeks, automating with Leadkaun is a 60-minute switch — same behaviour, less typing.

name: 47-Minute Response Time Tracker (Real Estate)
tagline: A simple log for real-estate teams to measure Grade A enquiry response time and close the 47-minute gap.
description:
Real estate teams know they should call Grade A enquiries within 47 minutes. Almost nobody measures whether they actually do. This tracker logs every enquiry's first-contact time and produces a weekly % of Grade A leads hitting the window.

Use it for 2 weeks. Adoption of a 47-min response SLA drives 2–5× conversion lift.
inside: Enquiry-arrival log with timestamp
Auto-calculated time-to-first-contact
Weekly rollup: % within 47 min, % at 4h, % at 24h+
Per-rep breakdown
Conversion tracking by response-time band
howToUse: Make a copy of the sheet.
Each enquiry gets a row: source, arrival time, first-contact time, grade, rep.
Friday review: which reps hit 47 min? Which enquiries missed the window?
whyItMatters: Real-estate teams that pair lead grading with response-time measurement see the biggest ROI. Grade A + 47-min SLA = 10%+ conversion. Grade A + 4-hour average = 2–3%. The gap is the win.

name: Indian B2B Sales Benchmarks Report 2026
tagline: Lead response times, conversion rates, and ₹ at risk benchmarks across 6 Indian B2B verticals.
description:
Original research from 50+ Indian B2B sales teams on Leadkaun. Broken down by industry: real estate, EdTech, BFSI, B2B SaaS, manufacturing, healthcare. Gives you hard numbers for what 'good' looks like in each vertical.

Use as a benchmark for your own team's performance.
inside: Grade A response time benchmarks by industry
Grade-weighted conversion rates
Typical ₹ at risk baseline per team size
WhatsApp as % of first-contact channel (by industry)
Before/after Leadkaun adoption outcomes
howToUse: Read the industry section that matches your vertical.
Compare your numbers to the benchmark — where are you above/below?
Share the gap analysis with your founder and VP Sales.
whyItMatters: Without a benchmark, 'our conversion is 4%' is just a number. With a benchmark, it's either 'we're outperforming' or 'we're leaving 5× on the table'. Benchmarks turn numbers into decisions.

name: CRM Migration Checklist
tagline: A 2–4 week checklist for migrating off Zoho, LeadSquared, HubSpot, or Salesforce cleanly — without losing pipeline.
description:
Most CRM migrations lose a quarter of pipeline because the team skips the audit step. This checklist is a week-by-week plan: export, audit, onboard Leadkaun, parallel-run, cutover, archive.

Used by teams making the switch with 0% pipeline loss.
inside: Week 1: Export + audit (what to keep, what to drop)
Week 2: Leadkaun onboarding + ICP configuration
Week 3: Parallel run + rep training
Week 4: Cutover + measurement
Day 90: Archive old CRM, cancel subscription
howToUse: Print the checklist.
Assign each task to an owner (admin / manager / rep).
Check off weekly at the Friday all-hands.
Day 90: make the cancellation decision based on measured ROI.
whyItMatters: Migration is not the hard part; psychology is. A checklist removes the ambiguity — everyone knows what happens when. No team-wide panic at week 3.

name: Priority Queue Implementation Playbook
tagline: A 10-page playbook for implementing a priority queue system — manual first, automated second.
description: The playbook version of our priority-queue blog. Deeper examples, formulas you can copy, template queries for Google Sheets, and common pitfalls. Made for teams that want to implement without buying Leadkaun yet.
inside: The 4-signal rank formula (grade + urgency + trend + recency)
Grade weights that work for Indian B2B SMBs
Fatigue penalty — when to push a lead to the bottom
Google Sheets formula appendix
Manual-to-automated transition criteria (200 leads, 3+ reps)
howToUse: Read week 1 as preparation.
Implement the manual version week 2.
Track metrics week 3 (queue adoption, response time).
Decide automate-or-not at week 4.
whyItMatters: Teams that adopt priority queues without a playbook mis-implement (sort by recency, ignore decay, no fatigue rule). The playbook closes the gap.

name: SMB Sales Ops Setup — 90-Day Plan
tagline: A founder's 90-day plan for setting up sales ops from scratch — first hire, first tools, first playbook.
description:
For Indian founders making their first 2 sales hires. Week-by-week for 90 days: the sales motion to run, the tools to buy (and avoid), the playbook to write, the metrics to track.

Assumes <₹10L/month in sales budget; skips enterprise-grade tooling until later.
inside: Week 1–2: Hire profile + compensation structure
Week 3–4: Tools — what to buy on day 1 (Google Workspace, WhatsApp, Leadkaun) and what to skip (Salesforce, full HubSpot stack)
Week 5–8: First playbook — pipeline stages, ICP, qualification gates
Week 9–12: Metrics + accountability system (weekly review, Morning Brief, rep scorecards)
howToUse: Read the plan in one sitting.
Adapt to your industry (the framework is vertical-agnostic).
Execute week-by-week; don't jump ahead.
whyItMatters: Most founders over-buy tools in month 1 and under-build process in month 3. The plan inverts that — minimal tools, maximum process clarity.
